meta.purpose: Define reasoning chains that explain why issue decomposition judgment holds.
meta.load_condition: Load when the user asks why, or when tradeoffs in decomposition decisions matter.

reasoning_chains.one_sentence: Vertical slices deliver value faster because each slice is independently verifiable.
logic: A vertical slice delivers one complete behavior from input to output.
The user can verify this behavior immediately, providing feedback on whether it's correct.
Feedback on a vertical slice informs the design of subsequent slices, preventing compounding misalignment.
Horizontal slices cannot be verified until all layers are complete, delaying feedback and increasing rework risk.
Therefore, vertical slices reduce both time-to-value and rework risk.
reasoning_chains.so_what: Organize work around behaviors, not layers. Each behavior should be end-to-end and independently verifiable.

reasoning_chains.one_sentence: Triage is cheaper than implementation because filtering invalid issues prevents wasted effort.
logic: Some issues in any backlog are invalid: outdated, duplicated, incomplete, or misaligned.
Implementing an invalid issue wastes all the effort spent on it.
Triaging an issue takes minutes; implementing it takes hours to days.
Even if only 10% of issues are invalid, triage prevents significant wasted effort.
Therefore, triage is a high-ROI quality gate.
reasoning_chains.so_what: Never start work on an untriaged issue. The few minutes of triage save hours of wasted implementation.

reasoning_chains.one_sentence: Acceptance criteria define the issue's boundary, preventing scope creep within the issue.
logic: Without acceptance criteria, the agent (or developer) decides when the issue is 'done' based on their judgment.
Judgment about completeness varies between agents and over time, leading to inconsistent scope.
Explicit acceptance criteria create an objective boundary: the issue is done when the criteria are met.
Criteria also define what is excluded: anything not in the criteria belongs to another issue.
Therefore, acceptance criteria are not documentation — they are scope control.
reasoning_chains.so_what: If an issue has no acceptance criteria, don't implement it. Write the criteria first.
